program.name: Telstra Graduate Program
program.salary_text: Competitive starting salary (no figure published). Includes Telstra Additional Day each year on top of four weeks’ annual leave, paid volunteer and cultural days, and discounts on Telstra products and partner offers.
program.duration_text: 14-month Graduate Program including a month-long induction; program commencement beginning February 2027.
program.rotation_text: Graduates are allocated into Chapters/Operative groups and assigned to missions/projects to develop capabilities in chosen pathways.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be eligible to work in Australia. International students with a valid visa that enables them to work in Australia without any work restrictions are eligible to apply to technical pathways only (Software Engineering, Network Engineering, Information Cyber & Security, Technology Consulting & Solutions).
eligibility.graduation_window: Must have completed a minimum undergraduate degree at an Australian university within the last three years before the Program commencement in February 2027 (i.e., graduating in 2024, 2025 or 2026). Mature-age students who have just graduated are eligible if they complete their degree by end of 2026.
minimum_requirements: Completed a minimum of an undergraduate degree within the eligibility window described above.
Be eligible to work in Australia (see visa restrictions for international students above).
You will need to upload a resume and your most recent academic transcript as part of the application.
If you are still waiting for final results you can apply using your GPA or unofficial transcript; official transcripts will be requested if you progress to interviews.

stages.name: Submit application
stages.details: Short online application form; upload resume and most recent academic transcript.

stages.name: Video interview & online assessment
stages.details: Video interview and online assessment completed via HireVue.

stages.name: Final interview (virtual) — three parts
stages.details: 1) Graduate ambassador Q&A session (meet current graduates); 2) Case study (present a 3–5 minute pitch on a topical issue provided beforehand); 3) Behavioural interview (motivational and behavioural questions).

stages.name: Checks
stages.details: Security/background checks (and medical assessment in some cases) before final offer.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: Successful candidates receive a phone call and a verbal/contract offer.

tips: For the HireVue video interview: use a quiet space, working camera and microphone, stable internet, and check lighting and background.
You will have practice opportunities in the video interview and up to three attempts per question—use them to prepare.
Read Telstra’s strategy and the role description for your chosen pathway so you can speak to why you’re a fit.
If invited to an interview, research the business unit and interviewer where possible and prepare thoughtful questions.
If you need adjustments or accessibility support, Telstra offers recruitment support and will work with you to make arrangements.

exec_themes: Network leadership and capacity: continuing heavy investment in mobile (5G), fibre (including intercity routes) and subsea capacity to keep Telstra’s network position and support higher data traffic.
Customer experience and digital transformation: improving Net Promoter Scores and reducing complaint referrals, expanding digital services and fraud/scam protections (e.g. Cleaner Pipes).
Cost discipline and organisational simplification: management actions to reduce cost and simplify the organisation announced in May 2024, with ongoing focus on efficiency.
Growth in enterprise, wholesale and platform services: monetise network assets (InfraCo/wholesale), expand enterprise offerings and build data & AI capabilities (including a JV with Accenture).
Sustainability as business priority: large renewable energy PPAs, emissions reduction targets and device recycling/repair programs integrated into strategy.
commercial_context.profit_engine: Telstra’s financial performance is driven mainly by network-based services: consumer mobile and fixed broadband revenue, enterprise and wholesale services, and earnings from network infrastructure (including fibre/subsea/infra monetisation). Incremental growth and margin expansion are sought from higher-value products and platform/data services (notably data/AI initiatives and enterprise solutions).
commercial_context.headwinds: Key headwinds called out in official reports include regulatory and competitive pressure in Australian retail and wholesale markets, the capital intensity and cost of network build and upgrades (including inflation and supply-chain pressures), and climate/physical risks to network assets that can cause unplanned repair and resilience costs.
commercial_context.esg: Telstra positions ESG as core to operations: targets include a 70% reduction in scope 1+2 emissions by 2030 (from FY19 baseline) and scope 3 reduction targets, a goal to source renewable energy equivalent to 100% of consumption (major PPAs and renewables investments), and circular-economy commitments such as device recycling and a 2030 goal to re-use/recycle one device for every two sold. Telstra publishes annual sustainability reports and a Modern Slavery Statement.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: Since FY24 Telstra has signalled a dual pivot: (1) a stronger focus on cost and organisational simplification announced 21 May 2024; and (2) an explicit push into data and AI capability (including a proposed global AI joint venture with Accenture announced 15 January 2025) to accelerate platform and service-based revenue beyond traditional connectivity.

notes: Duration, start month/year (February 2027), program length (14 months) and general benefits are explicitly stated on Telstra's Graduate Program page.
No explicit salary figure or list of office locations is published on the Telstra Graduate Program page; only descriptive phrases about salary and benefits are given.

Direct 'apply' link on the Telstra page points to Telstra's external careers/job system (telstra.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com); because that domain is outside the allowed set for this extraction, direct_apply_url is returned as null.
Eligibility rules, graduation window, visa restrictions for international students and requirements to upload transcripts are explicitly stated on the Graduate Program page.
The graduation window is defined relative to program commencement in February 2027 (graduating in 2024–2026).

The Students & Grads application process steps (Submit application; Video interview & online assessment via HireVue; Final virtual interview with three parts; Checks; Offers) are listed on the Graduate Program page and duplicated with more detail on the 'How we hire' page.
Interview preparation tips in the 'tips' section are taken from Telstra's 'How we hire' guidance for Students & Grads.

All statements are drawn from Telstra's official investor pages, annual report(s) and sustainability hub. Where wording in the source is organisational or thematic rather than a single label (for example 'profit engine'), the answer synthesises language used across investor and annual report materials rather than introducing new facts.

Profit composition (consumer mobile/fixed, enterprise, wholesale/infra, and growth via data/platforms) is synthesised from segment and strategy disclosure; Telstra does not publish a single-line 'profit engine' label.
Headwinds combine explicit risk disclosures (including climate-related risk) with management commentary in market updates — these are factual summaries of reported risks and guidance.
